summary: Round-2: the roi_ledger_promotion exclusion fix fully closes round 1's HIGH finding. normalizeCanonicalAuditSourceData now excludes both pr_merge.canonical_audit and pr_merge.roi_ledger_promotion, matching the only two fields merge-manager.js injects between promotion passes (verified by re-enumerating merge-object writes in lines 330-430; status/stop_reason change only on a failure branch outside this path). Test helper now models pass-2-only field presence with the real shape. Reviewer independently ran focused suite at ca76367c: 9/9 pass. Rebase commit is a 0-diff no-op for both risk-surface files (identical sha256), so recorded evidence stays content-accurate.

inspection.summary: Read the fix diff, re-enumerated all merge-object field writes between the two promotion passes in merge-manager.js, verified test helper shape/timing parity with merge-manager.js:390, self-ran focused tests at current head, compared surface-file hashes across the rebase.
inspection.evidence: node --test test/canonical-audit-idempotent-persistence.test.js (9/9 at ca76367c, run by reviewer); git diff 0e21970f ca76367c -- src/canonical-audit.js src/merge-manager.js (0 lines)

judgment_delta: initial: the exclusion list might still miss other fields injected between passes -> final: pass, because re-enumerating every merge-object write between the two passes confirmed canonical_audit and roi_ledger_promotion are the only injected fields and the regression test now fails without the fix
